title: A733 user manual SMHC/MBUS register audit
question: Does the public A733 user manual reveal a source-backed SDMMC0 IDMAC access-control or setup delta that should be tested?

findings.evidence: Manual section 11.3.5.4 documents 16-byte chained descriptors, 32-bit/bus-width alignment, DES0 OWN at bit 31, and DLBA as a 4-byte word address.
findings.impact: Matches the already-tested mainline/BSP descriptor model; no new descriptor geometry patch.

findings.evidence: Manual transfer procedures repeatedly say reset SMHC_CTRL[DMA_RST], write SMHC_IDMAC=0x82, configure IDIE, FIFOTH, DLBA, BYTCNT, descriptor, then launch command.
findings.impact: 0x82 equals FIX_BURST|IDMA_ON and matches the vendor/mainline path already tested; no new DMAC control patch.

findings.evidence: Manual lists MBUS_MAT_CLK_GATING_REG, MBUS_GATE_EN_REG, and AHB_MAT_CLK_GATING_REG bitfields. These name IOMMU, media, GMAC, NAND, DMA, CE, etc., but no SMHC0/SMHCx master gate.
findings.impact: Closes the obvious MBUS master-gate hypothesis for SMHC0; there is no SMHC bit to enable.

findings.evidence: Manual documents CCMU_SEC_SWITCH_REG at 0x1F00 with PLL/BUS/MBUS security bits; many clock registers are secure by default unless switched by secure firmware.
findings.impact: This supports the firmware/security-handoff blocker class, but it is secure-world controlled and not an upstream Linux SDMMC driver patch.

decision: Do not patch. The A733 user manual confirms the tested IDMAC sequence and shows no SMHC MBUS/AHB gate. The only remaining new detail is secure-world CCMU security switching, which requires firmware/maintainer evidence before any runtime diagnostic.
next_responsible_step: If maintainers/vendor mention CCMU_SEC_SWITCH_REG or secure firmware handoff, build a read-only diagnostic around CCU security/readability first; otherwise keep waiting/source acquisition.
